mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during economic expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and business confidence, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline returns (Sharpe ratio < 0.5) or retail sales and housing starts diverge without affecting equity prices.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals capacity constraints and impending profit margin compression, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases or the signal reverses direction during early-cycle recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets absorb inflation spikes without negative forward returns or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing months (accuracy < 50%).

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer demand, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero correlation with quarterly equity returns or Rank IC remains negative across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling real GDP growth and rising retail sales indicates unsustainable consumer borrowing that precedes a demand collapse, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.0% while RSAFS increases
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative returns or retail sales growth continues to support equity valuations despite slowing GDP.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable inflation during a post-recession recovery phase confirms supply chain normalization and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains fail to translate into positive 5-day returns during recovery phases or inflation spikes negate the supply signal.
